source_pr.title: *: fix data race caused by analyzeStatus.
source_pr.body:
<!-- Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close #15510，close #15521 <!-- REMOVE this line if no issue to close -->

Problem Summary:

### What is changed and how it works?

Proposal: [xxx](url) <!-- REMOVE this line if not applicable -->

What's Changed:
The global variable `analyzeStatus ` which not be locked in `analyze_jobs.go:89` caused data race among the UT.
Now locked it.

symptoms: PR body: analyzeStatus caused data race in UT; needs locking when reading updateTime.
failure_signature: PR body: analyzeStatus caused data race in UT; needs locking when reading updateTime.
root_cause_explanation: The code accessed analyzeStatus/analyze job fields without consistently holding the job mutex, which could trigger data races when multiple goroutines read/sort/update job state in tests. This PR fixes the race by ensuring updateTime/state are accessed via locked getters (e.g., job.getUpdateTime()).
fix_pattern: Ensure analyze job fields (e.g., updateTime) are read under the job mutex (use locked getters) to avoid data races.

evidence: evidence: PR body: analyzeStatus caused data race in UT; needs locking when reading updateTime.
evidence: diff changes sort comparator to call `job.getUpdateTime()` which locks job.Mutex before reading updateTime (statistics/analyze_jobs.go).

analysis:
